questions.question: Explain the difference between function declaration and function definition with examples.
expectedPoints: Declaration provides function signature without implementation
Definition includes the actual code implementation
Example: int add(int a, int b); vs int add(int a, int b) { return a+b; }

questions.question: Compare and contrast pass by value, pass by reference, and pass by pointer with suitable examples. Discuss when each method should be used.
expectedPoints: Pass by value: creates copy, original unchanged
Pass by reference: uses original variable, changes reflected
Pass by pointer: uses address, changes reflected with dereferencing
Use value for small data and when original shouldn't change
Use reference for large objects and when modification needed
Use pointers for optional parameters and dynamic memory
